natural_expression: Every Tuesday and Thursday at 6:30 AM, I meet my frontend colleague Chen Yu at the Binjiang running track for an unwavering 10km LSD long run, where we keep a close eye on each other's pace charts so neither of us dares to slack off.
After work, I often head to the Leke Fitness on Wensan Road, which is just 500 meters from the office. It's perfect for my push/pull/legs strength split, and I can go straight home to rest afterward.
My Nike Vaporfly 3s are my race-day shoes; I wore them to set my full marathon PB of 3:45. I can't bear to wear them during regular training, sticking to my Asics Kayano 30s instead.

Whenever I come home, my mother, Zhang Hui, always makes a point of stewing beef or steaming fish to boost my protein intake. Although my father, Lin Jianguo, used to worry that running would hurt my knees, now that he sees my medals, he even asks if I need to buy any nutritional supplements.

The iliotibial band syndrome in my right knee in 2023 forced me to stop training for two months. Since then, I've never dared to blindly lift heavy weights again; instead, I've immersed myself in sports anatomy, realizing deeply that recovery is more important than training.

Although I've stopped buying personal training sessions, I often discuss movement details with Wang Qiang, whom I know at the gym. When attempting heavy squats at 1.5 times my body weight, I still rely on him to spot me.

When the sheer number of code bugs before a project launch gets frustrating, I put on my Shokz bone conduction headphones and hit a HIIT session. Only when my body is utterly exhausted does my mind finally clear and calm down.

I'm in the habit of manually logging my weekly squat and deadlift weights and sets in Excel to analyze my progressive overload curve, while my daily running data syncs to my Garmin watch and Strava community for check-ins.

On weekdays, I bring my own bento boxes with brown rice and chicken breast, strictly controlling oil and salt intake. It's only after a long-distance run on Saturday night that I allow myself a cheat meal as a reward.

Sunday afternoons are reserved for my active recovery. I typically use a massage gun to relax the outer thighs, followed by yoga or contrast baths, to recharge for the work and training ahead on Monday.
Finishing the Hangzhou Marathon for the first time in 2021 was a turning point in my life. From that moment on, I no longer ran just to lose weight; I truly embraced my identity as a 'runner'.

Every morning after waking up at 6:30, I start with a cup of black coffee on an empty stomach, followed by 30 minutes of core activation or cardio. This is my switch to kickstart a highly productive day.

Whenever I feel mentally overwhelmed by tricky code bugs before a project launch, I immediately put on my noise-canceling headphones and go for a high-intensity interval run. I use extreme physical exhaustion to completely clear my mind.

The moment my Garmin watch showed that I had broken my half-marathon personal record, that intense sense of control made me eager to log it on Strava and enjoy the social validation from fellow runners' likes.

The severe pain from Iliotibial Band Syndrome (ITBS) in my right knee in 2023 still haunts me. Now, before increasing the weight on squats, the fear of having to stop training again makes me double-check my form details and ensure my warm-up is sufficient.

Seeing my father, Lin Jianguo, go from worrying that running would hurt my knees to proudly showing off my marathon medal to the neighbors fills me with a sense of pride in being understood by my family that feels even warmer than completing a heavy deadlift.

After enjoying a carefully planned cheat meal on Saturday night, the satisfaction of indulging following a week of strict oil and salt control makes every gram of brown rice and chicken breast I ate beforehand feel completely worth it.

On a Tuesday morning at the Binjiang running track, seeing Chen Yu's determined eyes closely chasing the pace chart instantly dispelled my lazy urge to slow down, replaced by a strong sense of responsibility not to let my teammate down.

When Wang Qiang shouted 'Up!' and securely caught the barbell as I attempted a 1.5x bodyweight squat, the excitement of precisely controlling that power confirmed that I am steadily approaching my goal.

Returning home to the aroma of beef stew cooked by my mother, Zhang Hui, gives me a sense of security from unconditional support, reminding me that no matter how exhausting work or training becomes, I always have a solid backbone behind me.

Late at night, opening an Excel spreadsheet and watching the progressive overload curve for strength training steadily rise gives me a profound sense of order—where logic prevails and effort guarantees returns. As a programmer, this is the moment I enjoy most.

During Sunday afternoon contrast baths, feeling my muscles transition from tension to relaxation brings a deep tranquility that completely empties the mind and body. It is the perfect soothing reward after a week of high-intensity training.

Standing at the starting line in my Nike Vaporfly 3, recalling the transformation from my first marathon in 2021, that strong sense of identity as 'a runner' makes me ignore the distance beneath my feet; I only want to keep moving forward.

At 6:30 AM, taking the first sip of black coffee on an empty stomach, I feel the caffeine kick in and my core activation begin. That switch from sleepiness to alertness fills me with a sense of control over the day ahead.

The first thing I do every morning after waking up at 6:30 AM is brew a cup of black coffee on an empty stomach. This is not just for a energy boost; it is my fixed ritual to kickstart 30 minutes of core activation or aerobic training.

As soon as I put on my Shokz OpenRun bone conduction headphones, I automatically slip into training mode. Whether it's the wind along the riverside track or the clanging of weights in the gym, it all becomes background noise that helps me focus on my pace and form.

After every 90-minute strength session at Lefits, no matter how exhausted I am, I immediately shake up and drink a scoop of ON whey protein. This is the golden window for muscle recovery, and I never miss it.

After every run, my first instinct isn't to stretch; it's to lift my wrist and confirm that my Garmin Forerunner 265 has successfully synced. Only when I see the route map generated on Strava do I feel that the workout is truly complete.

Since my injury in 2023, before attempting any heavy squats, I spend two minutes double-checking my belt tightness and ensuring my knees are properly warmed up. I also make eye contact with Wang Qiang to confirm he's in position to spot me before I even unrack the bar.

Sunday evening is my non-negotiable meal prep time. I portion out brown rice, chicken breast, and beef into five lunch boxes, ensuring I have strictly controlled, low-oil, low-salt healthy meals ready for every weekday lunch.

After every training session, I immediately use my massage gun to focus on releasing the iliotibial band on the outer thigh and my calves. This routine has become muscle memory, as I'm determined to prevent the injuries from 2023 from making a comeback.

Only after completing my long run on Saturday morning do I feel justified in scheduling a cheat meal for the evening. This 'work first, play later' rhythm allows me to enjoy good food without any guilt.

To ensure I fall asleep promptly at 23:00 for optimal muscle growth, I strictly put away my phone and stop coding at 22:00. This schedule is non-negotiable; no matter how busy the project gets, I never stay up late.

I maintain a strict rotation discipline for my running shoes: I wear only the Asics Kayano 30 for daily LSD training, saving my prized Nike Vaporfly 3 exclusively for race days or high-intensity workouts.

Whenever I break a personal record or complete a long-distance run on Strava, I always screenshot the data and share it on my social media feed. This isn't just about logging progress; it's about reinforcing my identity as a runner through social validation.
Taking a multivitamin and creatine with meals is my daily fixed routine, much like a dependency library in code; without them, my body's system cannot run efficiently.

The painful memory of my right knee's Iliotibial Band Syndrome (ITBS) in 2023 makes me格外 focused every Sunday when using a massage gun to relax the outer thigh. This reverence for recovery directly led me to develop a strict habit of falling asleep at 23:00 on weekdays, ensuring my muscles can fully repair during deep sleep.

To achieve peak performance while wearing Nike Vaporfly 3 shoes in my attempt to break the 1-hour-40-minute barrier in the half marathon, I must build sufficient endurance by wearing Asics Kayano 30 during Saturday long-distance runs. The cheat meal on Sunday after these runs serves as a necessary energy compensation for the week's grueling preparation.

Tuesday morning LSD runs with Chen Yu at the Binjiang track rely not only on mutual pace supervision to overcome inertia but also on the desire not to fall behind in front of a teammate. This peer pressure allows me to stay calm during Wednesday's high-pressure project periods, extending this self-discipline even to late-night Excel training data analysis.

The high-quality protein from the beef stew meticulously prepared by my mother, Zhang Hui, combined with my father, Lin Jianguo's, shift from worry to pride which eliminated my psychological burden, together empowered me to confidently ask Wang Qiang for a spot at the gym as I aimed to hit my limit goal of squatting 1.5 times my body weight.

When code bugs before a project launch trigger anxiety, I immediately put on my Shokz bone conduction headphones for a HIIT session. This mechanism of trading physical exhaustion for mental clarity allows me to quickly return to a rational state afterward, sorting through code logic as clearly as analyzing a progressive overload curve.

After syncing running data from my Garmin watch to Strava, I cross-analyze it with the strength training load curves recorded in Excel. Once signs of cumulative fatigue are detected, I immediately adjust the following week's plan and increase the frequency of contrast baths to prevent a recurrence of the injuries I suffered in 2023.

The 'runner' identity established during the 2021 Hangzhou Marathon served as my spiritual pillar, enabling me to persist through rehabilitation after being sidelined for two months due to ITBS in 2023. This belief ensures that every morning at 6:30 AM, when I drink black coffee on an empty stomach, I feel anticipation for the day's training rather than dread.

Wang Qiang's precise spotting while I attempted heavy squats gave me enough confidence to refine my movement details. This technical improvement, in turn, allowed me to provide my father, Lin Jianguo, with more scientific and convincing anatomical evidence when addressing his questions about supplement safety.

The strict discipline of bringing brown rice and chicken breast bento boxes every weekday is aimed at accumulating enough 'metabolic credit.' This allows me to enjoy a cheat meal with a clear conscience after completing a long-distance run on Saturday, and that sense of satisfaction becomes a powerful motivator to maintain a healthy diet for the following week.

It is precisely because I strictly rotate my Asics Kayano 30 during regular training to protect my feet that I ensure the Nike Vaporfly 3 is in peak condition on race day. This meticulous care for my gear mirrors the rigor I apply to code architecture, ultimately helping me continuously break personal records on Strava and gain social recognition.

Black coffee and core activation at 6:30 AM inject high efficiency into my workday, enabling me to clock off at 7:30 PM and head to Lefits Gym for a high-quality strength session. This, in turn, ensures I have enough energy before bed for foam rolling, creating a perfect closed loop from awakening to recovery.

As an engineer with a computer science background from Zhejiang University, I treat calorie data in MyFitnessPal as input parameters and load curves in Excel as algorithm outputs. This quantitative mindset allows me to precisely adjust my intake of ON protein powder and creatine, much like debugging code, steadily approaching my goal of squatting 1.5 times my body weight.
